title: Add reset-aware latent tyre-state features instead of more raw tyre-life ratios
summary: Recent F1 tire-degradation work models latent pace/degradation as a hidden state with pit stops acting as structural resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tire-degradation work models latent pace/degradation as a hidden state with pit stops acting as structural resets. Your current features use observed `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, and `Cumulative_Degradation`, but not an inferred latent 'tyre health' or 'abnormal wear' state. That may be the missing nonlinear signal near pit windows.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race-stint, fit simple train-fold-only state estimates such as: expected lap-time drift by compound and stint age, residualized degradation slope, EWMA of lap-time delta, and deviation from the expected post-pit reset curve. Feed only the resulting latent-state summaries, not a full sequence model, into the existing tabular learner.
expected_effect: Likely to sharpen discrimination in borderline cases where raw tyre age is similar but hidden wear state differs; especially useful on compounds/tracks with nonlinear deg curves.
risk: If the latent state is estimated using race-level information too aggressively, it can create hidden leakage; keep the estimator causal within each sequence.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
